recipes.title: Sautéed Swiss Chard with Garlic and Lemon
recipes.description: A simple, fast, healthy side dish that highlights fresh chard with garlic, lemon, and olive oil.

steps.title: Prep and separate
steps.description: Separate chard leaves from stems. Chop stems into 1/2-inch pieces. Roughly chop leaves.
steps.visual: Stems on one pile, leaves in another

steps.title: Sauté stems
steps.description: Heat olive oil in a large pot. Add stems and cook 5-6 minutes until glossy and softened.
steps.visual: Stems should be glossy and slightly tender

steps.title: Add garlic
steps.description: Add sliced garlic and red pepper flakes. Cook 1 minute until fragrant.
steps.visual: Garlic should be lightly golden, not browned

steps.title: Wilt leaves
steps.description: Add chopped leaves in batches, stirring until wilted. Add 1/4 cup water, cover, cook 5-7 minutes.
steps.visual: Leaves should be bright green and tender

steps.title: Finish
steps.description: Stir in lemon juice and zest. Season with salt and pepper.
steps.visual: Glistening, vibrant green with a fresh aroma

visual_guide.prep_shot: Fresh chard on the counter, stems and leaves visible before chopping
visual_guide.mid_cook: White stems glossy in the pan before adding leaves
visual_guide.finished: Tender green chard finished with lemon zest and garlic

photos.alt: Fresh Swiss chard with white stalks on a kitchen countertop
photos.caption: Fresh chard before cooking: separate thick stems from tender leaves.

photos.alt: Bunch of fresh chard ready to wash, trim, and chop
photos.caption: Prep checkpoint: wash well, then cut stems and leaves separately.
